item.title: Mexico Inflation Rose Slightly Less Than Expected in March
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation accelerated last month after the central bank resumed its easing cycle and marked up inflation forecasts with the Iran war pressuring energy costs globally.

item.title: Poland Keeps Rates on Hold to Gauge Iran War Impact on Inflation
item.description: The Polish central bank left interest rates unchanged on Thursday as policymakers try to assess the impact of the Iran war on inflation following the announcement of a fragile truce in the fighting.

item.title: Marsh’s Mercer Raises $3.8 Billion for Private Investments
item.description: Marsh & McLennan Cos’ Mercer has raised just over $3.8 billion for private investments.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CoreWeave, Instacart, Texas Instruments
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.3% as of 7:55 a.m. in New York as optimism about the ceasefire in the Middle East wavers.

item.title: Sleijpen Says ECB Will Act If Needed to Keep Inflation at Target
item.description: The European Central Bank is prepared to respond if the Iran war drives inflation away from its 2% target, according to Governing Council member Olaf Sleijpen.

item.title: Samsung to Invest $4 Billion in Chip Packaging Site in Vietnam
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. plans a $4 billion outlay to build a chip packaging plant in northern Vietnam, as the country’s largest foreign investor expands its footprint in the country.

item.title: Earnings Optimism Enhances Stocks’ Allure as Iran Worries Ease
item.description: Investors that slashed their stock exposure as war flared in the Middle East may soon have a fresh reason for piling back into equities: the upcoming earnings season.

item.title: Philippines Says China Fired Flares in Airspace ‘Bullying’
item.description: The Philippines said Chinese forces fired flares directly at its coast guard aircraft over the Spratly Islands in the disputed South China Sea, calling the incident “a clear and deliberate act of bullying.”

item.title: Iran-War Pause Opens Door for Congo’s Maiden Eurobond Sale
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is rushing to sell its maiden dollar bonds, taking advantage of a ceasefire in the war between the US and Iran.

item.title: Oil Chaos Is Clearest in the Hunt for ASAP Barrels
item.description: With the market suffering a massive loss of supply, refiners are scouting the world for crude.

item.title: Coreweave Expands Meta Deal for AI Computing to $21 Billion
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. has expanded its deal to supply artificial intelligence computing power to Meta Platforms Inc. to $21 billion, building on a previous $14.2 billion agreement the companies struck in September.

item.title: Thailand Seizes $260 Million Assets Linked to Alleged Scammers
item.description: Thai authorities seized assets worth 8.3 billion baht ($260 million) belonging to people behind an alleged money-laundering network linked to transnational cyber scam operations in Cambodia.

item.title: Anthropic Is the Center of the AI Orbit at HumanX Conference
item.description: The AI company is the industry marker for startup founders and VCs

item.title: How ‘Attritable’ Systems Are Changing the Economics of Warfare
item.description: Defense procurement is undergoing a transformation as scalable weaponry and next-generation technologies redefine battlefield capabilities

item.title: Hormuz Crisis Gives Canada Chance to Ease Tensions With Trump
item.description: The oft-overlooked NATO member and US northern neighbor might join if there’s a ceasefire.

item.title: Venezuela Airport Reawakens in Early Sign of Economic Recovery
item.description: Empty faucets, shuttered storefronts, scant arrivals and departures.

item.title: US Lithium Developer to List on Nasdaq in $573 Million SPAC Deal
item.description: A US lithium developer is preparing to go public on the Nasdaq through a blank-check company in a deal that values the firm at roughly $573 million.

item.title: Stockholm Office Vacancies at Highest Level Since 1990s Crisis
item.description: Vacancy rates for office properties in Stockholm continued to worsen at the start of this year, in a worrying sign for Sweden’s landlords who just escaped a funding crunch but now face more operational challenges.

item.title: OECD Urges Reeves to Overhaul UK Tax System to Boost Growth
item.description: The OECD urged UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves to overhaul the tax system to make it less complex and help turn around Britain’s tepid growth rates.

item.title: TCS Tops Profit Estimates After Cost Cuts to Cope With IT Slump
item.description: Tata Consultancy Services Ltd.’s quarterly earnings topped analysts’ estimates after the information technology provider curbed costs to cope with slowing demand in an era of geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Nvidia Shares Near Level Where Technical Traders See a Breakout
item.description: After months of sluggish returns, Nvidia Corp.’s stock is rallying again and close to breaking out of its narrow trading range, which market technicians see as a bullish signal.

item.title: Reliance Caps Fuel Sales at $11 Per Pump Amid Growing Shortages
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. has capped fuel purchases across its retail outlets — limiting customers to about $11 worth of gasoline or diesel — as the Middle East crisis continues to choke off supplies despite a tentative ceasefire.

item.title: The Essential Reading List on Cuba’s Past, Present and Maybe Even Its Future
item.description: Cuba scholar Michael J. Bustamante on the four books and two newsletters that he relies on.

item.title: UAE Oil Boss Says Hormuz Still Shut With Iran Restricting Access
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed with Iran still restricting access and preventing energy flows to global markets, the head of the biggest oil producer in the United Arab Emirates said. Tehran says transits must be under its supervision.

item.title: Wall Street Still Loves Bitcoin Even if Fever Is Cooling
item.description: Morgan Stanley rolls out its first Bitcoin fund.

item.title: Riskier Hybrid Debt Makes Comeback as European Bond Sales Jump
item.description: A tentative ceasefire in the Iran conflict is fueling a rush to issue new bonds in Europe’s primary market, with Thursday on track for the busiest day since early February.

item.title: Emerging-Market Rebound Stalls as Iran Truce Doubts Lift Oil
item.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies fell for the first time in five days as a shaky start to the Middle East ceasefire again pushed up oil prices.

item.title: China EV Exports Jump to Record as Iran Oil Shock Entices Buyers
item.description: China’s exports of electric vehicles and hybrids more than doubled in March to a record as the global energy shock stemming from the Iran war renewed interest in EVs.

item.title: Private Markets’ Software Pain Is About to Get a Lot Worse
item.description: The reckoning facing private markets has only just begun with a wall of software debt set to mature

item.title: OpenAI Pauses Stargate UK Data Center Effort Citing Energy Costs
item.description: OpenAI is pausing its Stargate infrastructure project in the UK, citing the high cost of energy and regulatory environment.
